recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet discusses demand, capacity, and model efficiency in general. It does not say or imply that Codex or ChatGPT rate limits have reset or that usage is available again now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: From the surge in demand on Codex and many other services, it is clear that AI companies are going to go through a phase of demand outpacing supply. Two things will be most important, having the most capacity and having the most efficient models. I believe we have both.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet discusses Codex behavior during a build process, not user caps, rate limits, or availability being restored.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Always fun when you notice Codex being clever in a way you don't expect. 

In a session today, it was running a slow build process and got annoyed (don't we all). Before making a change it checked that progress was actually happening and did so not by checking the logs, but by checking CPU usage.

recentEvaluations.rationale: Promotional tweet about Codex App adoption and install credits; no mention of limits, caps, or usage resetting.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
The Codex App is now our most used surface, ahead of the VS Code extension and the CLI. No wonder it inspires a few others 👀

You can install it here https://t.co/Lwg13vEJDn + you get up to $500 in credits if you are getting started as a business or enterprise.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet discusses traffic patterns and possible off-peak/peak usage incentives; it does not say Codex or ChatGPT limits/caps have reset or are available again now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
With Codex the there is quite the gulf in load between peak and off-peak times, and we would like to achieve more of a smoother traffic pattern as that would be a more optimal use of our compute. 

We have ideas, but curious what you all think we should do? Would more usage during off-peak and surge multiplier during peak times make sense?

automationEvents.rationale: The tweet explicitly says they are "resetting the usage limits for all plans," meaning rate/usage caps are available again now.
automationEvents.tweetText:
Our Codex dashboards are showing increased rate of users hitting rate limits and since we don't fully understand why I have made the cautious decision of resetting the usage limits for all plans. Enjoy.

I also wanted to celebrate us finding a pocket of fraudulent accounts that we banned and have helped us regain some compute. The fight against abuse never stops, but it's important to mark the moment and make it a little shared victory.
